1→//! Create multiple Light Mints and decompress all to Solana Mint accounts.
     2→//!
     3→//! This module provides functionality for batch creating Light Mints with
     4→//! optimal CPI batching. When creating multiple mints, it uses the CPI context
     5→//! pattern to minimize transaction overhead.
     6→//!
     7→//! # Flow
     8→//!
     9→//! - N=1: Single CPI (create + decompress)
    10→//! - N>1: 2N-1 CPIs (N-1 writes + 1 execute with decompress + N-1 decompress)
    11→
    12→use light_batched_merkle_tree::queue::BatchedQueueAccount;
    13→use light_compressed_account::instruction_data::traits::LightInstructionData;
    14→use light_compressed_token_sdk::compressed_token::mint_action::{
    15→    get_mint_action_instruction_account_metas_cpi_write, MintActionMetaConfig,
    16→    MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite,
    17→};
    18→use light_token_interface::{
    19→    instructions::{
    20→        extensions::{ExtensionInstructionData, TokenMetadataInstructionData},
    21→        mint_action::{
    22→            Action, CpiContext, CreateMint, DecompressMintAction,
    23→            MintActionCompressedInstructionData, MintInstructionData,
    24→        },
    25→    },
    26→    state::MintMetadata,
    27→    LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID,
    28→};
    29→use solana_account_info::AccountInfo;
    30→use solana_instruction::Instruction;
    31→use solana_program_error::ProgramError;
    32→use solana_pubkey::Pubkey;
    33→
    34→use super::SystemAccountInfos;
    35→
    36→/// Default rent payment epochs (~24 hours)
    37→pub const DEFAULT_RENT_PAYMENT: u8 = 16;
    38→/// Default lamports for write operations (~3 hours per write)
    39→pub const DEFAULT_WRITE_TOP_UP: u32 = 766;
    40→
    41→/// Parameters for a single mint within a batch creation.
    42→///
    43→/// Does not include proof since proof is shared across all mints in the batch.
    44→/// `mint` and `compression_address` are derived internally from `mint_seed_pubkey`.
    45→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    46→pub struct SingleMintParams<'a> {
    47→    pub decimals: u8,
    48→    pub mint_authority: Pubkey,
    49→    /// Optional mint bump. If `None`, derived from `find_mint_address(mint_seed_pubkey)`.
    50→    pub mint_bump: Option<u8>,
    51→    pub freeze_authority: Option<Pubkey>,
    52→    /// Mint seed pubkey (signer) for this mint. Used to derive `mint` PDA and `compression_address`.
    53→    pub mint_seed_pubkey: Pubkey,
    54→    /// Optional authority seeds for PDA signing
    55→    pub authority_seeds: Option<&'a [&'a [u8]]>,
    56→    /// Optional mint signer seeds for PDA signing
    57→    pub mint_signer_seeds: Option<&'a [&'a [u8]]>,
    58→    /// Optional token metadata for the mint (reference to avoid stack overflow)
    59→    pub token_metadata: Option<&'a TokenMetadataInstructionData>,
    60→}
    61→
    62→/// Parameters for creating one or more Light Mints with decompression.
    63→///
    64→/// Creates N Light Mints and decompresses all to Solana Mint accounts.
    65→/// Uses CPI context pattern when N > 1 for efficiency.
    66→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    67→pub struct CreateMintsParams<'a> {
    68→    /// Parameters for each mint to create
    69→    pub mints: &'a [SingleMintParams<'a>],
    70→    /// Single proof covering all new addresses
    71→    pub proof: light_compressed_account::instruction_data::compressed_proof::CompressedProof,
    72→    /// Root index for the address merkle tree (shared by all mints in batch).
    73→    pub address_merkle_tree_root_index: u16,
    74→    /// Rent payment in epochs for the Mint account (must be 0 or >= 2).
    75→    /// Default: 16 (~24 hours)
    76→    pub rent_payment: u8,
    77→    /// Lamports allocated for future write operations.
    78→    /// Default: 766 (~3 hours per write)
    79→    pub write_top_up: u32,
    80→    /// Offset for assigned_account_index when sharing CPI context with other accounts.
    81→    /// When creating mints alongside PDAs, this offset should be set to the number of
    82→    /// PDAs already written to the CPI context.
    83→    /// Default: 0 (no offset)
    84→    pub cpi_context_offset: u8,
    85→    /// Index of the output queue in tree accounts.
    86→    /// Default: 0
    87→    pub output_queue_index: u8,
    88→    /// Index of the address merkle tree in tree accounts.
    89→    /// Default: 1
    90→    pub address_tree_index: u8,
    91→    /// Index of the state merkle tree in tree accounts.
    92→    /// Required for decompress operations (discriminator validation).
    93→    /// Default: 2
    94→    pub state_tree_index: u8,
    95→}
    96→
    97→impl<'a> CreateMintsParams<'a> {
    98→    #[inline(never)]
    99→    pub fn new(
   100→        mints: &'a [SingleMintParams<'a>],
   101→        proof: light_compressed_account::instruction_data::compressed_proof::CompressedProof,
   102→        address_merkle_tree_root_index: u16,
   103→    ) -> Self {
   104→        Self {
   105→            mints,
   106→            proof,
   107→            address_merkle_tree_root_index,
   108→            rent_payment: DEFAULT_RENT_PAYMENT,
   109→            write_top_up: DEFAULT_WRITE_TOP_UP,
   110→            cpi_context_offset: 0,
   111→            output_queue_index: 0,
   112→            address_tree_index: 1,
   113→            state_tree_index: 2,
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    pub fn with_rent_payment(mut self, rent_payment: u8) -> Self {
   118→        self.rent_payment = rent_payment;
   119→        self
   120→    }
   121→
   122→    pub fn with_write_top_up(mut self, write_top_up: u32) -> Self {
   123→        self.write_top_up = write_top_up;
   124→        self
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    /// Set offset for assigned_account_index when sharing CPI context.
   128→    ///
   129→    /// Use this when creating mints alongside PDAs. The offset should be
   130→    /// the number of accounts already written to the CPI context.
   131→    pub fn with_cpi_context_offset(mut self, offset: u8) -> Self {
   132→        self.cpi_context_offset = offset;
   133→        self
   134→    }
   135→
   136→    /// Set the output queue index in tree accounts.
   137→    pub fn with_output_queue_index(mut self, index: u8) -> Self {
   138→        self.output_queue_index = index;
   139→        self
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    /// Set the address merkle tree index in tree accounts.
   143→    pub fn with_address_tree_index(mut self, index: u8) -> Self {
   144→        self.address_tree_index = index;
   145→        self
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    /// Set the state merkle tree index in tree accounts.
   149→    /// Required for decompress operations (discriminator validation).
   150→    pub fn with_state_tree_index(mut self, index: u8) -> Self {
   151→        self.state_tree_index = index;
   152→        self
   153→    }
   154→}
   155→
   156→/// CPI struct for on-chain programs to create multiple mints.
   157→///
   158→/// Uses named account fields for clarity and safety - no manual index calculations.
   159→///
   160→/// # Example
   161→///
   162→/// ```rust,ignore
   163→/// use light_token::instruction::{CreateMintsCpi, CreateMintsParams, SingleMintParams, SystemAccountInfos};
   164→///
   165→/// let params = CreateMintsParams::new(vec![mint_params_1, mint_params_2], proof);
   166→///
   167→/// CreateMintsCpi {
   168→///     mint_seeds: vec![mint_signer1.clone(), mint_signer2.clone()],
   169→///     payer: payer.clone(),
   170→///     address_tree: address_tree.clone(),
   171→///     output_queue: output_queue.clone(),
   172→///     compressible_config: config.clone(),
   173→///     mints: vec![mint_pda1.clone(), mint_pda2.clone()],
   174→///     rent_sponsor: rent_sponsor.clone(),
   175→///     system_accounts: SystemAccountInfos { ... },
   176→///     cpi_context_account: cpi_context.clone(),
   177→///     params,
   178→/// }.invoke()?;
   179→/// ```
   180→pub struct CreateMintsCpi<'a, 'info> {
   181→    /// Mint seed accounts (signers) - one per mint
   182→    pub mint_seed_accounts: &'a [AccountInfo<'info>],
   183→    /// Fee payer (also used as authority)
   184→    pub payer: AccountInfo<'info>,
   185→    /// Address tree for new mint addresses
   186→    pub address_tree: AccountInfo<'info>,
   187→    /// Output queue for compressed accounts
   188→    pub output_queue: AccountInfo<'info>,
   189→    /// State merkle tree (required for decompress discriminator validation)
   190→    pub state_merkle_tree: AccountInfo<'info>,
   191→    /// CompressibleConfig account
   192→    pub compressible_config: AccountInfo<'info>,
   193→    /// Mint PDA accounts (writable) - one per mint
   194→    pub mints: &'a [AccountInfo<'info>],
   195→    /// Rent sponsor PDA
   196→    pub rent_sponsor: AccountInfo<'info>,
   197→    /// Standard Light Protocol system accounts
   198→    pub system_accounts: SystemAccountInfos<'info>,
   199→    /// CPI context account
   200→    pub cpi_context_account: AccountInfo<'info>,
   201→    /// Parameters
   202→    pub params: CreateMintsParams<'a>,
   203→}
   204→
   205→impl<'a, 'info> CreateMintsCpi<'a, 'info> {
   206→    /// Validate that the struct is properly constructed.
   207→    #[inline(never)]
   208→    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   209→        let n = self.params.mints.len();
   210→        if n == 0 {
   211→            return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
   212→        }
   213→        if self.mint_seed_accounts.len() != n {
   214→            return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
   215→        }
   216→        if self.mints.len() != n {
   217→            return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
   218→        }
   219→        Ok(())
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    /// Execute all CPIs to create and decompress all mints.
   223→    ///
   224→    /// Signer seeds are extracted from `SingleMintParams::mint_signer_seeds` and
   225→    /// `SingleMintParams::authority_seeds` for each CPI call (0, 1, or 2 seeds per call).
   226→    #[inline(never)]
   227→    pub fn invoke(self) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   228→        self.validate()?;
   229→        let n = self.params.mints.len();
   230→
   231→        // Use single mint path only when:
   232→        // - N=1 AND
   233→        // - No CPI context offset (no PDAs were written to CPI context first)
   234→        if n == 1 && self.params.cpi_context_offset == 0 {
   235→            self.invoke_single_mint()
   236→        } else {
   237→            self.invoke_multiple_mints()
   238→        }
   239→    }
   240→
   241→    /// Handle the single mint case: create + decompress in one CPI.
   242→    #[inline(never)]
   243→    fn invoke_single_mint(self) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   244→        let mint_params = &self.params.mints[0];
   245→        let (mint, bump) = get_mint_and_bump(mint_params);
   246→
   247→        let mint_data =
   248→            build_mint_instruction_data(mint_params, self.mint_seed_accounts[0].key, mint, bump);
   249→
   250→        let decompress_action = DecompressMintAction {
   251→            rent_payment: self.params.rent_payment,
   252→            write_top_up: self.params.write_top_up,
   253→        };
   254→
   255→        let instruction_data = MintActionCompressedInstructionData::new_mint(
   256→            self.params.address_merkle_tree_root_index,
   257→            self.params.proof,
   258→            mint_data,
   259→        )
   260→        .with_decompress_mint(decompress_action);
   261→
   262→        let mut meta_config = MintActionMetaConfig::new_create_mint(
   263→            *self.payer.key,
   264→            *self.payer.key,
   265→            *self.mint_seed_accounts[0].key,
   266→            *self.address_tree.key,
   267→            *self.output_queue.key,
   268→            *self.compressible_config.key,
   269→            *self.rent_sponsor.key,
   270→        )
   271→        .with_compressible_mint(
   272→            *self.mints[0].key,
   273→            *self.compressible_config.key,
   274→            *self.rent_sponsor.key,
   275→        );
   276→        meta_config.input_queue = Some(*self.output_queue.key);
   277→
   278→        self.invoke_mint_action(instruction_data, meta_config, 0)
   279→    }
   280→
   281→    /// Handle the multiple mints case: N-1 writes + 1 execute + N-1 decompress.
   282→    #[inline(never)]
   283→    fn invoke_multiple_mints(self) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   284→        let n = self.params.mints.len();
   285→
   286→        // Get base leaf index before any CPIs modify the queue
   287→        let base_leaf_index = get_base_leaf_index(&self.output_queue)?;
   288→
   289→        let decompress_action = DecompressMintAction {
   290→            rent_payment: self.params.rent_payment,
   291→            write_top_up: self.params.write_top_up,
   292→        };
   293→
   294→        // Write mints 0..N-2 to CPI context
   295→        for i in 0..(n - 1) {
   296→            self.invoke_cpi_write(i)?;
   297→        }
   298→
   299→        // Execute: create last mint + decompress it
   300→        self.invoke_execute(n - 1, &decompress_action)?;
   301→
   302→        // Decompress remaining mints (0..N-2)
   303→        for i in 0..(n - 1) {
   304→            self.invoke_decompress(i, base_leaf_index, &decompress_action)?;
   305→        }
   306→
   307→        Ok(())
   308→    }
   309→
   310→    /// Invoke a CPI write instruction for a single mint.
   311→    /// Extracts signer seeds from mint params (0, 1, or 2 seeds).
   312→    #[inline(never)]
   313→    fn invoke_cpi_write(&self, index: usize) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   314→        let mint_params = &self.params.mints[index];
   315→        let offset = self.params.cpi_context_offset;
   316→        let (mint, bump) = get_mint_and_bump(mint_params);
   317→
   318→        // When sharing CPI context with PDAs:
   319→        // - first_set_context: only true for index 0 AND offset 0 (first write to context)
   320→        // - set_context: true if appending to existing context (index > 0 or offset > 0)
   321→        // - assigned_account_index: offset + index (to not collide with PDA indices)
   322→        let cpi_context = CpiContext {
   323→            set_context: index > 0 || offset > 0,
   324→            first_set_context: index == 0 && offset == 0,
   325→            in_tree_index: self.params.address_tree_index,
   326→            in_queue_index: self.params.output_queue_index,
   327→            out_queue_index: self.params.output_queue_index,
   328→            token_out_queue_index: 0,
   329→            assigned_account_index: offset + index as u8,
   330→            read_only_address_trees: [0; 4],
   331→            address_tree_pubkey: self.address_tree.key.to_bytes(),
   332→        };
   333→
   334→        let mint_data = build_mint_instruction_data(
   335→            mint_params,
   336→            self.mint_seed_accounts[index].key,
   337→            mint,
   338→            bump,
   339→        );
   340→
   341→        let instruction_data = MintActionCompressedInstructionData::new_mint_write_to_cpi_context(
   342→            self.params.address_merkle_tree_root_index,
   343→            mint_data,
   344→            cpi_context,
   345→        );
   346→
   347→        let cpi_write_config = MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite {
   348→            fee_payer: *self.payer.key,
   349→            mint_signer: Some(*self.mint_seed_accounts[index].key),
   350→            authority: *self.payer.key,
   351→            cpi_context: *self.cpi_context_account.key,
   352→        };
   353→
   354→        let account_metas = get_mint_action_instruction_account_metas_cpi_write(cpi_write_config);
   355→        let ix_data = instruction_data
   356→            .data()
   357→            .map_err(|e| ProgramError::BorshIoError(e.to_string()))?;
   358→
   359→        // Account order matches get_mint_action_instruction_account_metas_cpi_write:
   360→        // [0]: light_system_program
   361→        // [1]: mint_signer (optional, when present)
   362→        // [2]: authority
   363→        // [3]: fee_payer
   364→        // [4]: cpi_authority_pda
   365→        // [5]: cpi_context
   366→        let account_infos = [
   367→            self.system_accounts.light_system_program.clone(),
   368→            self.mint_seed_accounts[index].clone(),
   369→            self.payer.clone(),
   370→            self.payer.clone(),
   371→            self.system_accounts.cpi_authority_pda.clone(),
   372→            self.cpi_context_account.clone(),
   373→        ];
   374→        let instruction = Instruction {
   375→            program_id: Pubkey::new_from_array(LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID),
   376→            accounts: account_metas,
   377→            data: ix_data,
   378→        };
   379→
   380→        // Build signer seeds - pack present seeds at start of array
   381→        let mut seeds: [&[&[u8]]; 2] = [&[], &[]];
   382→        let mut num_signers = 0;
   383→        if let Some(s) = mint_params.mint_signer_seeds {
   384→            seeds[num_signers] = s;
   385→            num_signers += 1;
   386→        }
   387→        if let Some(s) = mint_params.authority_seeds {
   388→            seeds[num_signers] = s;
   389→            num_signers += 1;
   390→        }
   391→        solana_cpi::invoke_signed(&instruction, &account_infos, &seeds[..num_signers])
   392→    }
   393→
   394→    /// Invoke the execute instruction (create last mint + decompress).
   395→    /// Extracts signer seeds from mint params (0, 1, or 2 seeds).
   396→    #[inline(never)]
   397→    fn invoke_execute(
   398→        &self,
   399→        last_idx: usize,
   400→        decompress_action: &DecompressMintAction,
   401→    ) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   402→        let mint_params = &self.params.mints[last_idx];
   403→        let offset = self.params.cpi_context_offset;
   404→        let (mint, bump) = get_mint_and_bump(mint_params);
   405→
   406→        let mint_data = build_mint_instruction_data(
   407→            mint_params,
   408→            self.mint_seed_accounts[last_idx].key,
   409→            mint,
   410→            bump,
   411→        );
   412→
   413→        // Create struct directly to reduce stack usage (avoid builder pattern intermediates)
   414→        let instruction_data = MintActionCompressedInstructionData {
   415→            leaf_index: 0,
   416→            prove_by_index: false,
   417→            root_index: self.params.address_merkle_tree_root_index,
   418→            max_top_up: u16::MAX, // No limit
   419→            create_mint: Some(CreateMint::default()),
   420→            actions: vec![Action::DecompressMint(*decompress_action)],
   421→            proof: Some(self.params.proof),
   422→            cpi_context: Some(CpiContext {
   423→                set_context: false,
   424→                first_set_context: false,
   425→                in_tree_index: self.params.address_tree_index,
   426→                in_queue_index: self.params.address_tree_index,
   427→                out_queue_index: self.params.output_queue_index,
   428→                token_out_queue_index: 0,
   429→                assigned_account_index: offset + last_idx as u8,
   430→                read_only_address_trees: [0; 4],
   431→                address_tree_pubkey: self.address_tree.key.to_bytes(),
   432→            }),
   433→            mint: Some(mint_data),
   434→        };
   435→
   436→        let mut meta_config = MintActionMetaConfig::new_create_mint(
   437→            *self.payer.key,
   438→            *self.payer.key,
   439→            *self.mint_seed_accounts[last_idx].key,
   440→            *self.address_tree.key,
   441→            *self.output_queue.key,
   442→            *self.compressible_config.key,
   443→            *self.rent_sponsor.key,
   444→        )
   445→        .with_compressible_mint(
   446→            *self.mints[last_idx].key,
   447→            *self.compressible_config.key,
   448→            *self.rent_sponsor.key,
   449→        );
   450→        meta_config.cpi_context = Some(*self.cpi_context_account.key);
   451→        meta_config.input_queue = Some(*self.output_queue.key);
   452→
   453→        self.invoke_mint_action(instruction_data, meta_config, last_idx)
   454→    }
   455→
   456→    /// Invoke decompress for a single mint.
   457→    /// Extracts signer seeds from mint params (0, 1, or 2 seeds).
   458→    #[inline(never)]
   459→    fn invoke_decompress(
   460→        &self,
   461→        index: usize,
   462→        base_leaf_index: u32,
   463→        decompress_action: &DecompressMintAction,
   464→    ) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   465→        let mint_params = &self.params.mints[index];
   466→        let (mint, bump) = get_mint_and_bump(mint_params);
   467→
   468→        let mint_data = build_mint_instruction_data(
   469→            mint_params,
   470→            self.mint_seed_accounts[index].key,
   471→            mint,
   472→            bump,
   473→        );
   474→
   475→        let instruction_data = MintActionCompressedInstructionData {
   476→            leaf_index: base_leaf_index + index as u32,
   477→            prove_by_index: true,
   478→            root_index: 0,
   479→            max_top_up: u16::MAX, // No limit
   480→            create_mint: None,
   481→            actions: vec![Action::DecompressMint(*decompress_action)],
   482→            proof: None,
   483→            cpi_context: None,
   484→            mint: Some(mint_data),
   485→        };
   486→
   487→        // For prove_by_index, the tree_pubkey must be state_merkle_tree for discriminator validation
   488→        let meta_config = MintActionMetaConfig::new(
   489→            *self.payer.key,
   490→            *self.payer.key,
   491→            *self.state_merkle_tree.key, // tree_pubkey - state merkle tree for discriminator check
   492→            *self.output_queue.key,      // input_queue
   493→            *self.output_queue.key,      // output_queue
   494→        )
   495→        .with_compressible_mint(
   496→            *self.mints[index].key,
   497→            *self.compressible_config.key,
   498→            *self.rent_sponsor.key,
   499→        );
   500→
   501→        self.invoke_mint_action(instruction_data, meta_config, index)
   502→    }
   503→
   504→    /// Invoke a mint action instruction.
   505→    /// Extracts signer seeds from mint params at the given index (0, 1, or 2 seeds).
   506→    #[inline(never)]
   507→    fn invoke_mint_action(
   508→        &self,
   509→        instruction_data: MintActionCompressedInstructionData,
   510→        meta_config: MintActionMetaConfig,
   511→        mint_index: usize,
   512→    ) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   513→        let account_metas = meta_config.to_account_metas();
   514→        let ix_data = instruction_data
   515→            .data()
   516→            .map_err(|e| ProgramError::BorshIoError(e.to_string()))?;
   517→
   518→        // Collect all account infos needed for the CPI
   519→        let mut account_infos = vec![self.payer.clone()];
   520→
   521→        // System accounts
   522→        account_infos.push(self.system_accounts.light_system_program.clone());
   523→
   524→        // Add all mint seeds
   525→        for mint_seed in self.mint_seed_accounts {
   526→            account_infos.push(mint_seed.clone());
   527→        }
   528→
   529→        // More system accounts
   530→        account_infos.push(self.system_accounts.cpi_authority_pda.clone());
   531→        account_infos.push(self.system_accounts.registered_program_pda.clone());
   532→        account_infos.push(self.system_accounts.account_compression_authority.clone());
   533→        account_infos.push(self.system_accounts.account_compression_program.clone());
   534→        account_infos.push(self.system_accounts.system_program.clone());
   535→
   536→        // CPI context, queues, trees
   537→        account_infos.push(self.cpi_context_account.clone());
   538→        account_infos.push(self.output_queue.clone());
   539→        account_infos.push(self.state_merkle_tree.clone());
   540→        account_infos.push(self.address_tree.clone());
   541→        account_infos.push(self.compressible_config.clone());
   542→        account_infos.push(self.rent_sponsor.clone());
   543→
   544→        // Add all mint PDAs
   545→        for mint in self.mints {
   546→            account_infos.push(mint.clone());
   547→        }
   548→
   549→        let instruction = Instruction {
   550→            program_id: Pubkey::new_from_array(LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID),
   551→            accounts: account_metas,
   552→            data: ix_data,
   553→        };
   554→
   555→        // Build signer seeds - pack present seeds at start of array
   556→        let mint_params = &self.params.mints[mint_index];
   557→        let mut seeds: [&[&[u8]]; 2] = [&[], &[]];
   558→        let mut num_signers = 0;
   559→        if let Some(s) = mint_params.mint_signer_seeds {
   560→            seeds[num_signers] = s;
   561→            num_signers += 1;
   562→        }
   563→        if let Some(s) = mint_params.authority_seeds {
   564→            seeds[num_signers] = s;
   565→            num_signers += 1;
   566→        }
   567→        solana_cpi::invoke_signed(&instruction, &account_infos, &seeds[..num_signers])
   568→    }
   569→}
   570→
   571→/// Get mint PDA and bump, deriving mint always and bump if None.
   572→#[inline(never)]
   573→fn get_mint_and_bump(params: &SingleMintParams) -> (Pubkey, u8) {
   574→    let (mint, derived_bump) = super::find_mint_address(&params.mint_seed_pubkey);
   575→    let bump = params.mint_bump.unwrap_or(derived_bump);
   576→    (mint, bump)
   577→}
   578→
   579→/// Build MintInstructionData for a single mint.
   580→///
   581→/// `mint` and `bump` are derived externally from `mint_seed_pubkey` using `get_mint_and_bump`.
   582→#[inline(never)]
   583→fn build_mint_instruction_data(
   584→    mint_params: &SingleMintParams<'_>,
   585→    mint_signer: &Pubkey,
   586→    mint: Pubkey,
   587→    bump: u8,
   588→) -> MintInstructionData {
   589→    // Convert token_metadata to extensions if present
   590→    let extensions = mint_params
   591→        .token_metadata
   592→        .cloned()
   593→        .map(|metadata| vec![ExtensionInstructionData::TokenMetadata(metadata)]);
   594→
   595→    MintInstructionData {
   596→        supply: 0,
   597→        decimals: mint_params.decimals,
   598→        metadata: MintMetadata {
   599→            version: 3,
   600→            mint: mint.to_bytes().into(),
   601→            mint_decompressed: false,
   602→            mint_signer: mint_signer.to_bytes(),
   603→            bump,
   604→        },
   605→        mint_authority: Some(mint_params.mint_authority.to_bytes().into()),
   606→        freeze_authority: mint_params.freeze_authority.map(|a| a.to_bytes().into()),
   607→        extensions,
   608→    }
   609→}
   610→
   611→/// Get base leaf index from output queue account.
   612→#[inline(never)]
   613→fn get_base_leaf_index(output_queue: &AccountInfo) -> Result<u32, ProgramError> {
   614→    let queue = BatchedQueueAccount::output_from_account_info(output_queue)
   615→        .map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidAccountData)?;
   616→    Ok(queue.batch_metadata.next_index as u32)
   617→}
   618→
   619→/// Create multiple mints and decompress all to Solana accounts.
   620→///
   621→/// Convenience function that builds a [`CreateMintsCpi`] from a slice of accounts.
   622→///
   623→/// # Arguments
   624→///
   625→/// * `payer` - The fee payer account
   626→/// * `accounts` - The remaining accounts in the expected layout
   627→/// * `params` - Parameters for creating the mints
   628→///
   629→/// # Account Layout
   630→///
   631→/// - `[0]`: light_system_program
   632→/// - `[1..N+1]`: mint_signers (SIGNER)
   633→/// - `[N+1..N+6]`: system PDAs (cpi_authority, registered_program, compression_authority, compression_program, system_program)
   634→/// - `[N+6]`: cpi_context_account (writable)
   635→/// - `[N+7]`: output_queue (writable)
   636→/// - `[N+8]`: state_merkle_tree (writable)
   637→/// - `[N+9]`: address_tree (writable)
   638→/// - `[N+10]`: compressible_config
   639→/// - `[N+11]`: rent_sponsor (writable)
   640→/// - `[N+12..2N+12]`: mint_pdas (writable)
   641→/// - `[2N+12]`: compressed_token_program (for CPI)
   642→#[inline(never)]
   643→pub fn create_mints<'a, 'info>(
   644→    payer: &AccountInfo<'info>,
   645→    accounts: &'info [AccountInfo<'info>],
   646→    params: CreateMintsParams<'a>,
   647→) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
   648→    if params.mints.is_empty() {
   649→        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
   650→    }
   651→
   652→    let n = params.mints.len();
   653→    let mint_signers_start = 1;
   654→    let cpi_authority_idx = n + 1;
   655→    let registered_program_idx = n + 2;
   656→    let compression_authority_idx = n + 3;
   657→    let compression_program_idx = n + 4;
   658→    let system_program_idx = n + 5;
   659→    let cpi_context_idx = n + 6;
   660→    let output_queue_idx = n + 7;
   661→    let state_merkle_tree_idx = n + 8;
   662→    let address_tree_idx = n + 9;
   663→    let compressible_config_idx = n + 10;
   664→    let rent_sponsor_idx = n + 11;
   665→    let mint_pdas_start = n + 12;
   666→
   667→    // Build named struct from accounts slice
   668→    let cpi = CreateMintsCpi {
   669→        mint_seed_accounts: &accounts[mint_signers_start..mint_signers_start + n],
   670→        payer: payer.clone(),
   671→        address_tree: accounts[address_tree_idx].clone(),
   672→        output_queue: accounts[output_queue_idx].clone(),
   673→        state_merkle_tree: accounts[state_merkle_tree_idx].clone(),
   674→        compressible_config: accounts[compressible_config_idx].clone(),
   675→        mints: &accounts[mint_pdas_start..mint_pdas_start + n],
   676→        rent_sponsor: accounts[rent_sponsor_idx].clone(),
   677→        system_accounts: SystemAccountInfos {
   678→            light_system_program: accounts[0].clone(),
   679→            cpi_authority_pda: accounts[cpi_authority_idx].clone(),
   680→            registered_program_pda: accounts[registered_program_idx].clone(),
   681→            account_compression_authority: accounts[compression_authority_idx].clone(),
   682→            account_compression_program: accounts[compression_program_idx].clone(),
   683→            system_program: accounts[system_program_idx].clone(),
   684→        },
   685→        cpi_context_account: accounts[cpi_context_idx].clone(),
   686→        params,
   687→    };
   688→    cpi.invoke()
   689→}

Validate the following implementation plan by reading the relevant source files in /Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol. Answer these specific questions:

1. Is it correct to charge the MINT_CREATION_FEE in write mode (write_to_cpi_context=true)?
2. Does the proposed account ordering in write mode make sense given the existing parse order in the program?
3. Is the sdk-libs/token-sdk pattern actually feasible with rent_sponsor in the write-mode CPI?

Also identify:
- Any open questions
- Any conflicting objectives

## Plan to validate

A new `MINT_CREATION_FEE = 50_000 lamports` was added. It charges the fee from `fee_payer` to `rent_sponsor` on mint creation. A restriction was added in `AccountsConfig::new` that blocks `create_mint` when `write_to_cpi_context = true`. This breaks `test_create_two_mints` because multi-mint creation uses the CPI context write/execute pattern.

### Proposed fix

**Step 1**: Remove the `create_mint` restriction in `AccountsConfig::new` (the block that returns `CpiContextSetNotUsable` when `create_mint.is_some() && write_to_cpi_context`).

**Step 2**: In write mode, also parse `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` before `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts` when `config.create_mint` is true. Add them as new optional fields on `MintActionAccounts`:
```
write_mode_compressible_config: Option<&'info CompressibleConfig>
write_mode_rent_sponsor: Option<&'info AccountInfo>
```

**Step 3**: In `processor.rs`, update fee charging to handle both write mode (use `write_mode_rent_sponsor` + `fee_payer` from `write_to_cpi_context_system`) and execute mode (use `executing.rent_sponsor` + `executing.system.fee_payer`).

**Step 4**: Update the SDK's `invoke_cpi_write` in `sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` to include `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` before `fee_payer` in the write-mode account list.

**Step 5**: Update `cpi_accounts_start_offset` in the program to account for the new accounts in write mode.

## Files to read

Read ALL of these before answering:

1. `programs/compressed-token/program/src/compressed_token/mint_action/accounts.rs` — full file
2. `programs/compressed-token/program/src/compressed_token/mint_action/processor.rs` — full file
3. `sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` — full file (or find equivalent path)
4. `sdk-libs/compressed-token-sdk/src/compressed_token/v2/create_compressed_mint/instruction.rs` — full file
5. `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/tests/basic_test.rs` lines 635-740
6. `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/src/instruction_accounts.rs` lines 133-200

Search for `invoke_cpi_write` and `CreateMintsCpi` in sdk-libs to find all write-mode CPI builders.

- List open questions with suggested resolutions
- List conflicting objectives with suggested resolutions

content.content:
1→//! Batch create Light Mints CPI for pinocchio.
     2→//!
     3→//! Provides type aliases with pinocchio's `AccountInfo` already set,
     4→//! wrapping the generic types from `light_sdk_types`.
     5→//!
     6→//! # Flow
     7→//!
     8→//! - N=1 (no CPI context offset): Single CPI (create + decompress)
     9→//! - N>1 or offset>0: 2N-1 CPIs (N-1 writes + 1 execute with decompress + N-1 decompress)
    10→//!
    11→//! # Example
    12→//!
    13→//! ```rust,ignore
    14→//! use light_token_pinocchio::instruction::{
    15→//!     CreateMintsCpi, CreateMintsParams, SingleMintParams,
    16→//!     get_output_queue_next_index,
    17→//! };
    18→//!
    19→//! // Get base leaf index before any CPIs
    20→//! let base_leaf_index = get_output_queue_next_index(&output_queue)?;
    21→//!
    22→//! // mint and compression_address are derived internally from mint_seed_pubkey
    23→//! let mint_params = [SingleMintParams {
    24→//!     decimals: 9,
    25→//!     address_merkle_tree_root_index: root_index,
    26→//!     mint_authority: authority_key,
    27→//!     mint_bump: None, // derived from mint_seed_pubkey if None
    28→//!     freeze_authority: None,
    29→//!     mint_seed_pubkey: mint_seed_key,
    30→//!     authority_seeds: None,
    31→//!     mint_signer_seeds: Some(&mint_signer_seeds),
    32→//!     token_metadata: None,
    33→//! }];
    34→//!
    35→//! let params = CreateMintsParams {
    36→//!     mints: &mint_params,
    37→//!     proof,
    38→//!     rent_payment: 16,
    39→//!     write_top_up: 766,
    40→//!     cpi_context_offset: 0,
    41→//!     output_queue_index: 0,
    42→//!     address_tree_index: 1,
    43→//!     state_tree_index: 2,
    44→//!     base_leaf_index,
    45→//! };
    46→//!
    47→//! CreateMintsCpi {
    48→//!     mint_seed_accounts: &[&mint_seed_account],
    49→//!     payer: &payer,
    50→//!     address_tree: &address_tree,
    51→//!     output_queue: &output_queue,
    52→//!     state_merkle_tree: &state_merkle_tree,
    53→//!     compressible_config: &config,
    54→//!     mints: &[&mint_pda_account],
    55→//!     rent_sponsor: &rent_sponsor,
    56→//!     light_system_program: &light_system,
    57→//!     cpi_authority_pda: &cpi_authority,
    58→//!     registered_program_pda: &registered_program,
    59→//!     account_compression_authority: &compression_authority,
    60→//!     account_compression_program: &compression_program,
    61→//!     system_program: &system_program,
    62→//!     cpi_context_account: &cpi_context,
    63→//!     params,
    64→//! }.invoke()?;
    65→//! ```
    66→
    67→// Re-export non-generic types and functions directly
    68→pub use light_sdk_types::interface::cpi::create_mints::{
    69→    get_output_queue_next_index, CreateMintsParams, SingleMintParams, DEFAULT_RENT_PAYMENT,
    70→    DEFAULT_WRITE_TOP_UP,
    71→};
    72→use pinocchio::account_info::AccountInfo;
    73→
    74→/// High-level struct for creating compressed mints (pinocchio).
    75→///
    76→/// Type alias with pinocchio's `AccountInfo` already set.
    77→/// Consolidates proof parsing, tree account resolution, and CPI invocation.
    78→///
    79→/// # Example
    80→///
    81→/// ```rust,ignore
    82→/// CreateMints {
    83→///     mints: &sdk_mints,
    84→///     proof_data: &params.create_accounts_proof,
    85→///     mint_seed_accounts,
    86→///     mint_accounts,
    87→///     infra,
    88→/// }
    89→/// .invoke(&cpi_accounts)?;
    90→/// ```
    91→pub type CreateMints<'a> =
    92→    light_sdk_types::interface::cpi::create_mints::CreateMints<'a, AccountInfo>;
    93→
    94→/// CPI struct for creating multiple Light Mints (pinocchio).
    95→///
    96→/// Type alias with pinocchio's `AccountInfo` already set.
    97→pub type CreateMintsCpi<'a> =
    98→    light_sdk_types::interface::cpi::create_mints::CreateMintsCpi<'a, AccountInfo>;
    99→
   100→/// Infrastructure accounts for mint creation CPI (pinocchio).
   101→///
   102→/// Type alias with pinocchio's `AccountInfo` already set.
   103→pub type CreateMintsStaticAccounts<'a> =
   104→    light_sdk_types::interface::cpi::create_mints::CreateMintsStaticAccounts<'a, AccountInfo>;
   105→
   106→/// Find the mint PDA address for a given mint seed (pinocchio).
   107→///
   108→/// Returns `([u8; 32], u8)` -- the PDA address bytes and bump.
   109→pub fn find_mint_address(mint_seed: &[u8; 32]) -> ([u8; 32], u8) {
   110→    light_sdk_types::interface::cpi::create_mints::find_mint_address::<AccountInfo>(mint_seed)
   111→}
   112→
   113→/// Derive the Light Mint address from a mint seed and address tree pubkey (pinocchio).
   114→///
   115→/// This computes `derive_address(find_mint_address(mint_seed).0, address_tree, LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID)`.
   116→pub fn derive_mint_compressed_address(
   117→    mint_seed: &[u8; 32],
   118→    address_tree_pubkey: &[u8; 32],
   119→) -> [u8; 32] {
   120→    light_sdk_types::interface::cpi::create_mints::derive_mint_compressed_address::<AccountInfo>(
   121→        mint_seed,
   122→        address_tree_pubkey,
   123→    )
   124→}
   125→

635→async fn test_create_two_mints() {
   636→    use csdk_anchor_full_derived_test::instruction_accounts::{
   637→        CreateTwoMintsParams, MINT_SIGNER_A_SEED, MINT_SIGNER_B_SEED,
   638→    };
   639→    use light_token::instruction::{
   640→        find_mint_address as find_cmint_address, LIGHT_TOKEN_CONFIG, LIGHT_TOKEN_RENT_SPONSOR,
   641→    };
   642→
   643→    let ctx = shared::SharedTestContext::new_with_config(|config| {
   644→        config.with_decoders(vec![
   645→            Box::new(csdk_anchor_full_derived_test::CsdkTestInstructionDecoder),
   646→            Box::new(csdk_anchor_full_derived_test::CsdkAnchorFullDerivedTestInstructionDecoder),
   647→        ])
   648→    })
   649→    .await;
   650→
   651→    let shared::SharedTestContext {
   652→        mut rpc,
   653→        payer,
   654→        config_pda,
   655→        rent_sponsor: _,
   656→        program_id,
   657→    } = ctx;
   658→
   659→    let authority = Keypair::new();
   660→
   661→    // Derive PDAs for both mint signers
   662→    let (mint_signer_a_pda, mint_signer_a_bump) = Pubkey::find_program_address(
   663→        &[MINT_SIGNER_A_SEED, authority.pubkey().as_ref()],
   664→        &program_id,
   665→    );
   666→    let (mint_signer_b_pda, mint_signer_b_bump) = Pubkey::find_program_address(
   667→        &[MINT_SIGNER_B_SEED, authority.pubkey().as_ref()],
   668→        &program_id,
   669→    );
   670→
   671→    // Derive mint PDAs
   672→    let (cmint_a_pda, _) = find_cmint_address(&mint_signer_a_pda);
   673→    let (cmint_b_pda, _) = find_cmint_address(&mint_signer_b_pda);
   674→
   675→    // Get proof for both mints
   676→    let proof_result = get_create_accounts_proof(
   677→        &rpc,
   678→        &program_id,
   679→        vec![
   680→            CreateAccountsProofInput::mint(mint_signer_a_pda),
   681→            CreateAccountsProofInput::mint(mint_signer_b_pda),
   682→        ],
   683→    )
   684→    .await
   685→    .unwrap();
   686→
   687→    // Debug: Check proof contents
   688→    println!(
   689→        "proof_result.create_accounts_proof.proof.0.is_some() = {:?}",
   690→        proof_result.create_accounts_proof.proof.0.is_some()
   691→    );
   692→    println!(
   693→        "proof_result.remaining_accounts.len() = {:?}",
   694→        proof_result.remaining_accounts.len()
   695→    );
   696→
   697→    let accounts = csdk_anchor_full_derived_test::accounts::CreateTwoMints {
   698→        fee_payer: payer.pubkey(),
   699→        authority: authority.pubkey(),
   700→        mint_signer_a: mint_signer_a_pda,
   701→        mint_signer_b: mint_signer_b_pda,
   702→        cmint_a: cmint_a_pda,
   703→        cmint_b: cmint_b_pda,
   704→        compression_config: config_pda,
   705→        light_token_config: LIGHT_TOKEN_CONFIG,
   706→        light_token_rent_sponsor: LIGHT_TOKEN_RENT_SPONSOR,
   707→        light_token_program: LIGHT_TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID.into(),
   708→        light_token_cpi_authority: light_token_types::CPI_AUTHORITY_PDA.into(),
   709→        system_program: solana_sdk::system_program::ID,
   710→    };
   711→
   712→    let instruction_data = csdk_anchor_full_derived_test::instruction::CreateTwoMints {
   713→        params: CreateTwoMintsParams {
   714→            create_accounts_proof: proof_result.create_accounts_proof,
   715→            mint_signer_a_bump,
   716→            mint_signer_b_bump,
   717→        },
   718→    };
   719→
   720→    let instruction = Instruction {
   721→        program_id,
   722→        accounts: [
   723→            accounts.to_account_metas(None),
   724→            proof_result.remaining_accounts,
   725→        ]
   726→        .concat(),
   727→        data: instruction_data.data(),
   728→    };
   729→
   730→    rpc.create_and_send_transaction(&[instruction], &payer.pubkey(), &[&payer, &authority])
   731→        .await
   732→        .expect("CreateTwoMints should succeed");
   733→
   734→    // Verify both mints exist on-chain
   735→    let cmint_a_account = rpc
   736→        .get_account(cmint_a_pda)
   737→        .await
   738→        .unwrap()
   739→        .expect("Mint A should exist on-chain");
   740→    let cmint_b_account = rpc
   741→        .get_account(cmint_b_pda)
   742→        .await
   743→        .unwrap()
   744→        .expect("Mint B should exist on-chain");

content.text:
Now I have all the information needed.

---

## Validation Results

### Question 1: Is it correct to charge the MINT_CREATION_FEE in write mode (write_to_cpi_context=true)?

**YES, it is correct to charge the fee in write mode, but the current code cannot do it.**

The fee is charged via `transfer_lamports_via_cpi` which calls `SystemTransfer` -- a system program CPI requiring `fee_payer` (signer, writable) and `rent_sponsor` (writable). In write mode, the program currently only parses `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts` which contains `fee_payer`, `cpi_authority_pda`, and `cpi_context` (3 accounts). It does NOT parse `compressible_config` or `rent_sponsor`.

The fee transfer itself is a simple SOL transfer from fee_payer to rent_sponsor. It does not depend on executing the light-system CPI. So charging it during the write phase is semantically correct -- the fee should be charged once per mint creation, and the write phase is where `create_mint` data gets written.

However, charging during write mode means: if the execute phase later fails, the fee was already transferred but the mint was not created. This is acceptable because the entire transaction is atomic on Solana -- if any CPI fails, all state changes (including the transfer) are rolled back.

### Question 2: Does the proposed account ordering in write mode make sense given the existing parse order?

**NO, the proposed ordering has a problem.**

Looking at `validate_and_parse` (line 88-102 of accounts.rs), the write mode path is:

```
authority -> CpiContextLightSystemAccounts(fee_payer, cpi_authority_pda, cpi_context) -> assert empty
```

The plan proposes inserting `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` **before** `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts`. This means the parse order would be:

```
authority -> compressible_config -> rent_sponsor -> fee_payer -> cpi_authority_pda -> cpi_context
```

This is fine from the program's perspective since the iterator is sequential -- it just needs to match what the SDK sends. However, the plan must also update the `cpi_accounts_start_offset` for write mode. Currently the write-mode branch in `cpi_accounts_start_offset` (line 232-234) falls through to return `offset` without entering the `executing` block. The CPI accounts in write mode start at `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts`, so if we add 2 accounts before it, the offset must increase by 2.

Looking at `cpi_accounts_end_offset` (line 239-240), for write mode it returns `start_offset + CpiContextLightSystemAccounts::cpi_len()`. This is correct -- the CPI slice for write mode is just the 3 CpiContextLightSystemAccounts. The new `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` are NOT part of the CPI to light-system-program; they are consumed by the compressed-token program before the CPI.

So the ordering is feasible but `cpi_accounts_start_offset` must account for the 2 new optional accounts in write mode.

### Question 3: Is the sdk-libs/token-sdk pattern actually feasible with rent_sponsor in the write-mode CPI?

**YES, it is feasible but requires changes to both SDK implementations.**

Looking at `invoke_cpi_write` in `sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` (lines 293-377), the current write-mode account list is:

```
[0]: light_system_program
[1]: mint_signer
[2]: authority (payer)
[3]: fee_payer (payer)
[4]: cpi_authority_pda
[5]: cpi_context
```

This needs to become:

```
[0]: light_system_program
[1]: mint_signer
[2]: authority (payer)
[3]: compressible_config    <-- NEW
[4]: rent_sponsor           <-- NEW
[5]: fee_payer (payer)
[6]: cpi_authority_pda
[7]: cpi_context
```

Both `CreateMintsCpi` structs (in `sdk-types` and `token-sdk`) already have `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields, so no new fields are needed on the struct. The change is only in `invoke_cpi_write` to include these 2 accounts.

Similarly, the `token-sdk` version (`sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` lines 313-392) uses `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` which currently only has `fee_payer`, `mint_signer`, `authority`, `cpi_context`. This struct needs `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields added.

### Open Questions

1. **Should the "iterator is empty" check on line 91-93 be relaxed?** Currently in write mode, after parsing `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts`, it asserts no remaining accounts. With the new accounts, this check happens before the new accounts would be parsed, so the order of operations is: parse new accounts first, then parse CpiContextLightSystemAccounts, then check empty. This should work as proposed.

2. **Should the fee be charged once in write mode or once in execute mode?** The plan says charge in write mode using `write_mode_rent_sponsor`. For N mints, the write phase is called N-1 times and execute once. The fee should be charged per-mint (N times total), so it must be charged in both write and execute paths when `create_mint` is true. The plan's Step 3 mentions handling both modes, which is correct.

3. **What about the `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` struct?** The plan's Step 4 mentions updating `sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` but the `token-sdk` version in `sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` uses `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` from `light-compressed-token-sdk`. This struct also needs updating. The plan should explicitly list this.

### Conflicting Objectives

1. **Fee-per-write vs fee-per-execute**: Each write CPI creates one mint, so the fee should be charged per write CPI AND per execute CPI. The current code charges fee only when `accounts_config.create_mint` is true. In write mode with the restriction removed, `create_mint` will be true, so the fee charging code (lines 50-68 of processor.rs) will execute. But it currently requires `validated_accounts.executing` which is `None` in write mode. The plan correctly identifies this -- Step 3 must add a separate code path for write mode to get `rent_sponsor` and `fee_payer` from different sources.

2. **No real conflict**: The plan's objectives are aligned. The restriction was overly conservative; removing it and adding the necessary accounts is the right approach.

### Overall Assessment

**REVISE** with these specific changes:

1. **Step 2 correction**: The new fields should NOT be on `MintActionAccounts` as `write_mode_compressible_config` and `write_mode_rent_sponsor`. Instead, add them as parsed accounts in the write-mode branch of `validate_and_parse` (before `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts::new`). Store them directly on `MintActionAccounts` as optional fields (e.g. reuse the names `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` at the top level, not nested in `ExecutingAccounts`).

2. **Step 5 must be more specific**: `cpi_accounts_start_offset` needs a write-mode branch that adds the count of new optional accounts. Currently it only handles the `executing` case. Add logic like:
   ```
   if self.write_mode_compressible_config.is_some() { offset += 1; }
   if self.write_mode_rent_sponsor.is_some() { offset += 1; }
   ```

3. **Missing step**: The plan must update `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` in `sdk-libs/compressed-token-sdk` (wherever that struct is defined) to include `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields, and update `get_mint_action_instruction_account_metas_cpi_write` to emit the corresponding `AccountMeta` entries.

4. **Missing step**: Remove the `iter.iterator_is_empty()` check at line 91-93, or move it after parsing the new optional accounts. Currently it would fail if extra accounts are present.

5. **Step 3 needs more detail**: In write mode, `fee_payer` comes from `write_to_cpi_context_system.fee_payer` and `rent_sponsor` from the new `write_mode_rent_sponsor` field. The `compressible_config` validation (matching rent_sponsor to config) must also happen in write mode.

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/programs/compressed-token/program/src/compressed_token/mint_action/accounts.rs` - Core account parsing: remove restriction, add write-mode config/rent_sponsor parsing, update offsets
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/programs/compressed-token/program/src/compressed_token/mint_action/processor.rs` - Fee charging logic: add write-mode branch using new account fields
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` - Generic CPI builder: update invoke_cpi_write to include compressible_config and rent_sponsor
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` - Token SDK CPI: update invoke_cpi_write and MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite usage
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/programs/compressed-token/program/src/shared/accounts.rs` - CpiContextLightSystemAccounts struct reference for understanding write-mode CPI account layout

## Validation Results

### Question 1: Is it correct to charge the MINT_CREATION_FEE in write mode (write_to_cpi_context=true)?

**YES, it is correct to charge the fee in write mode, but the current code cannot do it.**

The fee is charged via `transfer_lamports_via_cpi` which calls `SystemTransfer` -- a system program CPI requiring `fee_payer` (signer, writable) and `rent_sponsor` (writable). In write mode, the program currently only parses `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts` which contains `fee_payer`, `cpi_authority_pda`, and `cpi_context` (3 accounts). It does NOT parse `compressible_config` or `rent_sponsor`.

The fee transfer itself is a simple SOL transfer from fee_payer to rent_sponsor. It does not depend on executing the light-system CPI. So charging it during the write phase is semantically correct -- the fee should be charged once per mint creation, and the write phase is where `create_mint` data gets written.

However, charging during write mode means: if the execute phase later fails, the fee was already transferred but the mint was not created. This is acceptable because the entire transaction is atomic on Solana -- if any CPI fails, all state changes (including the transfer) are rolled back.

### Question 2: Does the proposed account ordering in write mode make sense given the existing parse order?

**NO, the proposed ordering has a problem.**

Looking at `validate_and_parse` (line 88-102 of accounts.rs), the write mode path is:

```
authority -> CpiContextLightSystemAccounts(fee_payer, cpi_authority_pda, cpi_context) -> assert empty
```

The plan proposes inserting `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` **before** `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts`. This means the parse order would be:

```
authority -> compressible_config -> rent_sponsor -> fee_payer -> cpi_authority_pda -> cpi_context
```

This is fine from the program's perspective since the iterator is sequential -- it just needs to match what the SDK sends. However, the plan must also update the `cpi_accounts_start_offset` for write mode. Currently the write-mode branch in `cpi_accounts_start_offset` (line 232-234) falls through to return `offset` without entering the `executing` block. The CPI accounts in write mode start at `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts`, so if we add 2 accounts before it, the offset must increase by 2.

Looking at `cpi_accounts_end_offset` (line 239-240), for write mode it returns `start_offset + CpiContextLightSystemAccounts::cpi_len()`. This is correct -- the CPI slice for write mode is just the 3 CpiContextLightSystemAccounts. The new `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` are NOT part of the CPI to light-system-program; they are consumed by the compressed-token program before the CPI.

So the ordering is feasible but `cpi_accounts_start_offset` must account for the 2 new optional accounts in write mode.

### Question 3: Is the sdk-libs/token-sdk pattern actually feasible with rent_sponsor in the write-mode CPI?

**YES, it is feasible but requires changes to both SDK implementations.**

Looking at `invoke_cpi_write` in `sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` (lines 293-377), the current write-mode account list is:

```
[0]: light_system_program
[1]: mint_signer
[2]: authority (payer)
[3]: fee_payer (payer)
[4]: cpi_authority_pda
[5]: cpi_context
```

This needs to become:

```
[0]: light_system_program
[1]: mint_signer
[2]: authority (payer)
[3]: compressible_config    <-- NEW
[4]: rent_sponsor           <-- NEW
[5]: fee_payer (payer)
[6]: cpi_authority_pda
[7]: cpi_context
```

Both `CreateMintsCpi` structs (in `sdk-types` and `token-sdk`) already have `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields, so no new fields are needed on the struct. The change is only in `invoke_cpi_write` to include these 2 accounts.

Similarly, the `token-sdk` version (`sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` lines 313-392) uses `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` which currently only has `fee_payer`, `mint_signer`, `authority`, `cpi_context`. This struct needs `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields added.

### Open Questions

1. **Should the "iterator is empty" check on line 91-93 be relaxed?** Currently in write mode, after parsing `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts`, it asserts no remaining accounts. With the new accounts, this check happens before the new accounts would be parsed, so the order of operations is: parse new accounts first, then parse CpiContextLightSystemAccounts, then check empty. This should work as proposed.

2. **Should the fee be charged once in write mode or once in execute mode?** The plan says charge in write mode using `write_mode_rent_sponsor`. For N mints, the write phase is called N-1 times and execute once. The fee should be charged per-mint (N times total), so it must be charged in both write and execute paths when `create_mint` is true. The plan's Step 3 mentions handling both modes, which is correct.

3. **What about the `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` struct?** The plan's Step 4 mentions updating `sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` but the `token-sdk` version in `sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` uses `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` from `light-compressed-token-sdk`. This struct also needs updating. The plan should explicitly list this.

### Conflicting Objectives

1. **Fee-per-write vs fee-per-execute**: Each write CPI creates one mint, so the fee should be charged per write CPI AND per execute CPI. The current code charges fee only when `accounts_config.create_mint` is true. In write mode with the restriction removed, `create_mint` will be true, so the fee charging code (lines 50-68 of processor.rs) will execute. But it currently requires `validated_accounts.executing` which is `None` in write mode. The plan correctly identifies this -- Step 3 must add a separate code path for write mode to get `rent_sponsor` and `fee_payer` from different sources.

2. **No real conflict**: The plan's objectives are aligned. The restriction was overly conservative; removing it and adding the necessary accounts is the right approach.

### Overall Assessment

**REVISE** with these specific changes:

1. **Step 2 correction**: The new fields should NOT be on `MintActionAccounts` as `write_mode_compressible_config` and `write_mode_rent_sponsor`. Instead, add them as parsed accounts in the write-mode branch of `validate_and_parse` (before `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts::new`). Store them directly on `MintActionAccounts` as optional fields (e.g. reuse the names `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` at the top level, not nested in `ExecutingAccounts`).

2. **Step 5 must be more specific**: `cpi_accounts_start_offset` needs a write-mode branch that adds the count of new optional accounts. Currently it only handles the `executing` case. Add logic like:
   ```
   if self.write_mode_compressible_config.is_some() { offset += 1; }
   if self.write_mode_rent_sponsor.is_some() { offset += 1; }
   ```

3. **Missing step**: The plan must update `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite` in `sdk-libs/compressed-token-sdk` (wherever that struct is defined) to include `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields, and update `get_mint_action_instruction_account_metas_cpi_write` to emit the corresponding `AccountMeta` entries.

4. **Missing step**: Remove the `iter.iterator_is_empty()` check at line 91-93, or move it after parsing the new optional accounts. Currently it would fail if extra accounts are present.

5. **Step 3 needs more detail**: In write mode, `fee_payer` comes from `write_to_cpi_context_system.fee_payer` and `rent_sponsor` from the new `write_mode_rent_sponsor` field. The `compressible_config` validation (matching rent_sponsor to config) must also happen in write mode.

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/programs/compressed-token/program/src/compressed_token/mint_action/accounts.rs` - Core account parsing: remove restriction, add write-mode config/rent_sponsor parsing, update offsets
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/programs/compressed-token/program/src/compressed_token/mint_action/processor.rs` - Fee charging logic: add write-mode branch using new account fields
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/sdk-libs/sdk-types/src/interface/cpi/create_mints.rs` - Generic CPI builder: update invoke_cpi_write to include compressible_config and rent_sponsor
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/sdk-libs/token-sdk/src/instruction/create_mints.rs` - Token SDK CPI: update invoke_cpi_write and MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite usage
- `/Users/ananas/dev/light-protocol/programs/compressed-token/program/src/shared/accounts.rs` - CpiContextLightSystemAccounts struct reference for understanding write-mode CPI account layout

## Validation Summary

The plan is **REVISE** with these findings:

### Open Questions Resolved

1. **Charge fee in write mode?** YES — the fee transfer is a simple SOL transfer (atomic with the tx), so charging it per write-CPI call is correct. Each write CPI = one mint, so N mints = N fee charges (N-1 in write mode + 1 in execute mode).

2. **Account ordering in write mode** — the proposed ordering (authority → compressible_config → rent_sponsor → CpiContextLightSystemAccounts) is fine, but **`cpi_accounts_start_offset` must increment by the count of new optional accounts** in write mode.

3. **SDK feasibility** — YES, both `sdk-types` and `token-sdk` CPI builders already have `compressible_config`/`rent_sponsor` fields; they just need to emit them in `invoke_cpi_write`.

### Required Additions to the Plan

1. **Move the `iter.iterator_is_empty()` check** — currently asserts no remaining accounts right after `CpiContextLightSystemAccounts` in write mode; the new optional accounts must be parsed *before* this check, or the check must be relaxed.

2. **Update `MintActionMetaConfigCpiWrite`** in `sdk-libs/compressed-token-sdk` to include `compressible_config` and `rent_sponsor` fields in write-mode account metas.

3. **`cpi_accounts_start_offset` needs a write-mode branch** that adds +1 per new optional account present.

4. **Step 3 detail**: In write mode, `fee_payer` comes from `write_to_cpi_context_system.fee_payer`, and `rent_sponsor` from the new top-level `write_mode_rent_sponsor` field. Config validation (rent_sponsor key matches `compressible_config.rent_sponsor`) must also run in write mode.

Shall I proceed with the implementation?

1→use anchor_lang::solana_program::program_error::ProgramError;
     2→use pinocchio::account_info::AccountInfo;
     3→
     4→use crate::shared::AccountIterator;
     5→
     6→pub struct CpiContextLightSystemAccounts<'info> {
     7→    pub fee_payer: &'info AccountInfo,
     8→    pub cpi_authority_pda: &'info AccountInfo,
     9→    pub cpi_context: &'info AccountInfo,
    10→}
    11→
    12→impl<'info> CpiContextLightSystemAccounts<'info> {
    13→    /// Returns the number of accounts in the CPI context light system accounts slice
    14→    pub const fn cpi_len() -> usize {
    15→        3 // fee_payer, cpi_authority_pda, cpi_context
    16→    }
    17→
    18→    #[track_caller]
    19→    #[inline(always)]
    20→    pub fn new(iter: &mut AccountIterator<'info, AccountInfo>) -> Result<Self, ProgramError> {
    21→        Ok(Self {
    22→            fee_payer: iter.next_signer_mut("fee_payer")?,
    23→            cpi_authority_pda: iter.next_account("cpi_authority_pda")?,
    24→            cpi_context: iter.next_account("cpi_context")?,
    25→        })
    26→    }
    27→}
    28→
    29→pub struct LightSystemAccounts<'info> {
    30→    /// Fee payer account (index 0) - signer, mutable
    31→    pub fee_payer: &'info AccountInfo,
    32→    /// CPI authority PDA (index 1) - signer (via CPI)
    33→    pub cpi_authority_pda: &'info AccountInfo,
    34→    /// Registered program PDA (index 2) - non-mutable
    35→    pub registered_program_pda: &'info AccountInfo,
    36→    /// Account compression authority (index 3) - non-mutable
    37→    pub account_compression_authority: &'info AccountInfo,
    38→    /// Account compression program (index 4) - non-mutable
    39→    pub account_compression_program: &'info AccountInfo,
    40→    /// System program (index 5) - non-mutable
    41→    pub system_program: &'info AccountInfo,
    42→    /// Sol pool PDA (index 6) - optional, mutable if present
    43→    pub sol_pool_pda: Option<&'info AccountInfo>,
    44→    /// SOL decompression recipient (index 7) - optional, mutable, for SOL decompression
    45→    pub sol_decompression_recipient: Option<&'info AccountInfo>,
    46→    /// CPI context account (index 8) - optional, mutable
    47→    pub cpi_context: Option<&'info AccountInfo>,
    48→}
    49→
    50→impl<'info> LightSystemAccounts<'info> {
    51→    /// Returns the number of required accounts in the light system accounts slice (excludes optional accounts)
    52→    pub const fn cpi_len() -> usize {
    53→        6 // fee_payer, cpi_authority_pda, registered_program_pda, account_compression_authority, account_compression_program, system_program
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    #[track_caller]
    57→    pub fn validate_and_parse(
    58→        iter: &mut AccountIterator<'info, AccountInfo>,
    59→        with_sol_pool: bool,
    60→        decompress_sol: bool,
    61→        with_cpi_context: bool,
    62→    ) -> Result<Self, ProgramError> {
    63→        Ok(Self {
    64→            fee_payer: iter.next_signer_mut("fee_payer")?,
    65→            cpi_authority_pda: iter.next_non_mut("cpi_authority_pda")?,
    66→            registered_program_pda: iter.next_non_mut("registered_program_pda")?,
    67→            account_compression_authority: iter.next_non_mut("account_compression_authority")?,
    68→            account_compression_program: iter.next_non_mut("account_compression_program")?,
    69→            system_program: iter.next_non_mut("system_program")?,
    70→            sol_pool_pda: iter.next_option("sol_pool_pda", with_sol_pool)?,
    71→            sol_decompression_recipient: iter
    72→                .next_option("sol_decompression_recipient", decompress_sol)?,
    73→            cpi_context: iter.next_option_mut("cpi_context", with_cpi_context)?,
    74→        })
    75→    }
    76→}
    77→
